title: Global, Green and Information Systems Management
summary: Information Systems Management (ISM), IT outsourcing, offshoring, green technology, and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in the context of digital business.
content_html:
Global, Green, and Information Systems Management

Information Systems Management (ISM) focuses on managing critical and costly IS/IT systems across organisations as technology and business environments constantly change.

What ISM Involves

  • Managing IS to innovate operations and achieve competitive advantage.
  • Managing IS to support business processes and decision-making.
  • Managing IS to enable digital business models and social engagement.
  • Managing SDLC methods to deploy technology solutions.
  • Managing HR requirements of IS/IT portfolios.
  • Managing IS infrastructure to enable business applications and processes.

IT Outsourcing

Outsourcing: Allocation of specific business processes to a specialist external service provider. Almost every organisation outsources in some way. Typically the function outsourced is non-core to the business.

Most commonly outsourced: IT, legal services, content development, web design, recruitment, logistics, manufacturing, technical/customer support.

Reasons for Outsourcing

  • Cost reduction -- 'costs-out' strategy.
  • Access to specialised skills not available internally.
  • Focus on core business activities.
  • Time zone advantages.
  • Faster and better services from specialists.

Risks of Outsourcing

  • Risk of exposing confidential data and intellectual property.
  • Loss of internal knowledge and skills over time.
  • When IT becomes core to competitive advantage, outsourcing may need to be reconsidered.
  • Vendor dependency and contract management complexity.

Green Technology and SDGs

Green IT refers to environmentally sustainable computing -- reducing the environmental impact of technology. The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) provide a framework for addressing global challenges including climate change, inequality, and poverty. Digital technology can both contribute to and help solve environmental problems:

  • Positive: Remote work reduces commuting emissions; data centres moving to renewable energy; digital services replacing physical goods.
  • Negative: E-waste, energy consumption of data centres and crypto-mining, carbon footprint of manufacturing devices.

exercises.title: ISM definition
exercises.prompt: Information Systems Management (ISM) is concerned with:
options: Only building new software applications
Managing critical IS/IT systems as technology and business environments change
Exclusively managing cybersecurity threats
Replacing all IT with cloud services
exercises.answer: Managing critical IS/IT systems as technology and business environments change

exercises.title: Outsourcing definition
options: Hiring of internal IT staff from universities
Allocation of specific business processes to an external specialist service provider
Moving all servers to the cloud
Automating IT operations with AI
exercises.answer: Allocation of specific business processes to an external specialist service provider

exercises.title: Outsourcing driver
exercises.prompt: The primary driver of outsourcing from the 1990s onwards was:
options: Improving data security
Reducing costs -- the 'costs-out' management goal
Building in-house expertise
Avoiding government regulation

exercises.title: Outsourcing risk
exercises.prompt: When should an organisation reconsider outsourcing its IT?
options: When IT costs are rising
When IT becomes core to competitive advantage
When the outsourcing vendor is performing well
When the organisation expands internationally
exercises.answer: When IT becomes core to competitive advantage

exercises.title: Green IT negative
exercises.prompt: Which is a NEGATIVE environmental impact of digital technology?
options: Remote work reducing commuting emissions
E-waste and energy consumption of data centres
Digital services replacing physical goods
Renewable energy powering data centres
exercises.answer: E-waste and energy consumption of data centres

exercises.title: Full outsourcing threshold
exercises.prompt: Full outsourcing is defined as approximately:
options: 10% or more of IT requirements supported externally
50% or more of IT requirements supported externally
80% or more of business requirements supported by an external entity
100% cloud migration
exercises.answer: 80% or more of business requirements supported by an external entity

exercises.title: SDGs purpose
exercises.prompt: The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) provide:
options: A technical framework for building IS systems
A framework for addressing global challenges including climate change, inequality, and poverty
A regulatory framework for data privacy
A standard for measuring IT outsourcing performance
exercises.answer: A framework for addressing global challenges including climate change, inequality, and poverty

exercises.title: ISM HR responsibility
options: Only managing hardware procurement
Managing the Human Resource requirements of the IS/IT portfolio
Exclusively focusing on cybersecurity
Replacing business processes with automation
exercises.answer: Managing the Human Resource requirements of the IS/IT portfolio
